choicesItalian: Buongiorno, mi porta in Via Veneto?
choicesEnglish: Good morning, can you take me to Via Veneto?
Good morning, can you take me to the station?
Good morning, can you take me to the hotel?
Good morning, can you take me downtown?
correctAnswerItalian: Buongiorno, mi porta in Via Veneto?
correctAnswerEnglish: Good morning, can you take me to Via Veneto?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un modo classico per indicare la destinazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, Via Veneto è una strada famosa di Roma.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A classic way to indicate the destination.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, Via Veneto is a famous street in Rome.

choicesItalian: C'è la tariffa fissa di cinquanta euro.
C'è lo sconto del dieci per cento.
C'è un supplemento per il bagaglio.
choicesEnglish: There is a fixed rate of fifty euros.
There is a ten percent discount.
There is a surcharge for luggage.
There is a ten-minute wait.
correctAnswerItalian: C'è la tariffa fissa di cinquanta euro.
correctAnswerEnglish: There is a fixed rate of fifty euros.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Molte città hanno tariffe fisse dagli aeroporti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: Sbagliato. Si parla della tariffa fissa per il centro.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Many cities have fixed rates from airports.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. We are talking about the fixed rate for the center.

choicesEnglish: Do you accept credit cards?
Do you accept only cash?
Do you accept bank checks?
Do you accept foreign coins?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you accept credit cards?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere il metodo di pagamento prima di partire.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, carta di credito significa credit card.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for the payment method before leaving.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, credit card is carta di credito.

choicesItalian: Sì, ho il POS a bordo.
Sì, ma solo per grandi cifre.
choicesEnglish: Yes, I have the POS on board.
No, you must pay in cash.
Maybe, if the line works.
Yes, but only for large amounts.
correctAnswerItalian: Sì, ho il POS a bordo.
correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, I have the POS on board.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Molti taxi sono ormai attrezzati per i pagamenti elettronici.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, POS a bordo significa card machine on board.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Many taxis are now equipped for electronic payments.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, POS on board is POS a bordo.

choicesEnglish: Is there a lot of traffic today?
Is it very sunny today?
Are there many people today?
Is it very windy today?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is there a lot of traffic today?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un'osservazione comune durante il tragitto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: Sbagliato. Si chiede del traffico stradale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A common observation during the ride.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. You are asking about road traffic.

choicesEnglish: There has been a road accident.
There has been a big concert.
There has been a local market.
There has been a strong thunderstorm.
correctAnswerEnglish: There has been a road accident.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Spiega il motivo del possibile ritardo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Explains the reason for the possible delay.

choicesEnglish: We will take an alternative road.
We will take the usual fast road.
We will take the longest road.
We will take the most beautiful road.
correctAnswerEnglish: We will take an alternative road.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Il tassista cerca di evitare l'ingorgo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The taxi driver tries to avoid the traffic jam.

choicesEnglish: Maximum ten minutes delay.
Minimum one hour wait.
About two hours journey.
Exactly five minutes.
correctAnswerEnglish: Maximum ten minutes delay.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una stima del tempo perso nel traffico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An estimate of the time lost in traffic.

choicesEnglish: Alright, I'm not in a hurry.
No, I'm in a big hurry.
Sorry, but I have an appointment.
Quickly, I must arrive immediately!
correctAnswerEnglish: Alright, I'm not in a hurry.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Rassicura il tassista che non c'è urgenza.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Reassures the taxi driver that there's no urgency.

choicesEnglish: We have arrived. How much do I owe you?
We have left. How much does it cost?
We are halfway. How much longer?
We are close. Where are we going?
correctAnswerEnglish: We have arrived. How much do I owe you?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La frase per chiedere il totale alla fine della corsa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The phrase to ask for the total at the end of the ride.

choicesItalian: Tenga pure il resto come mancia.
Mi dia il resto, per favore.
Non ho monete per il resto.
Il resto è sbagliato, conti bene.
choicesEnglish: Keep the change as a tip.
Give me the change, please.
I don't have coins for the change.
The change is wrong, count well.
correctAnswerItalian: Tenga pure il resto come mancia.
correctAnswerEnglish: Keep the change as a tip.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un gesto di cortesia verso il tassista.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A gesture of courtesy towards the taxi driver.

choicesEnglish: Do you need a receipt?
Do you need help?
Do I need a bag?
Do you need the ticket?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you need a receipt?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Proposta standard alla fine del servizio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Standard offer at the end of the service.

choicesItalian: Sì, mi serve per il rimborso spese.
Sì, mi serve per il viaggio.
Sì, mi serve per la polizia.
Sì, mi serve per la mia famiglia.
choicesEnglish: Yes, I need it for expense reimbursement.
Yes, I need it for the trip.
Yes, I need it for the police.
Yes, I need it for my family.
correctAnswerItalian: Sì, mi serve per il rimborso spese.
correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, I need it for expense reimbursement.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Molti viaggiatori d'affari chiedono la ricevuta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Many business travelers ask for a receipt.

choicesItalian: È possibile prenotare un taxi per domani?
È possibile comprare un taxi per domani?
È possibile guidare un taxi per domani?
È possibile lavare un taxi per domani?
choicesEnglish: Is it possible to book a taxi for tomorrow?
Is it possible to buy a taxi for tomorrow?
Is it possible to drive a taxi for tomorrow?
Is it possible to wash a taxi for tomorrow?
correctAnswerItalian: È possibile prenotare un taxi per domani?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is it possible to book a taxi for tomorrow?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere una prenotazione anticipata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for an advance reservation.

choicesEnglish: Call our taxi dispatch.
Call the train station.
Call the airport right away.
Call your family now.
correctAnswerEnglish: Call our taxi dispatch.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Il tassista suggerisce il modo corretto per prenotare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, centrale radiotaxi significa taxi dispatch.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The taxi driver suggests the correct way to book.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, taxi dispatch is centrale radiotaxi.

choicesEnglish: Can you turn on the air conditioning?
Can you open the door?
Can you turn off the radio?
Can you stop here?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you turn on the air conditioning?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una richiesta comune per il comfort durante il viaggio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, aria condizionata significa air conditioning.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A common request for comfort during the ride.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, air conditioning is aria condizionata.

choicesItalian: Metta pure le valigie nel bagagliaio.
Metta pure le valigie sul tetto.
Metta pure le valigie sul sedile.
Metta pure le valigie per terra.
choicesEnglish: Feel free to put the suitcases in the trunk.
Feel free to put the suitcases on the roof.
Feel free to put the suitcases on the seat.
Feel free to put the suitcases on the ground.
correctAnswerItalian: Metta pure le valigie nel bagagliaio.
correctAnswerEnglish: Feel free to put the suitcases in the trunk.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il posto corretto per i bagagli ingombranti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The correct place for bulky luggage.

choicesItalian: Quanto tempo ci vuole per arrivare?
Quanto costa il viaggio in totale?
choicesEnglish: How long does it take to arrive?
How much does the trip cost in total?
Who is driving the taxi today?
Where are we now?
correctAnswerItalian: Quanto tempo ci vuole per arrivare?
correctAnswerEnglish: How long does it take to arrive?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere la durata prevista del tragitto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, tempo ci vuole significa how long it takes.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for the expected duration of the ride.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, how long it takes is quanto tempo ci vuole.

choicesItalian: Giri a destra al prossimo incrocio.
Giri a sinistra dopo la piazza.
choicesEnglish: Turn right at the next intersection.
Turn left after the square.
Go straight until the church.
Stop in front of the hotel.
correctAnswerItalian: Giri a destra al prossimo incrocio.
correctAnswerEnglish: Turn right at the next intersection.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Fornire indicazioni stradali al tassista.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Providing road directions to the taxi driver.

choicesItalian: Mi può dare una mano con il bagaglio?
Mi può dare un consiglio sulla città?
choicesEnglish: Can you give me a hand with the luggage?
Can you give me advice about the city?
Can you lend me an umbrella?
Can you call me a friend?
correctAnswerItalian: Mi può dare una mano con il bagaglio?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you give me a hand with the luggage?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere assistenza fisica per le valigie.
feedback.incorrectItalian: Sbagliato. Dare una mano significa help.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for physical assistance with the suitcases.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. Give a hand means to help.

choicesItalian: Non posso, ho mal di schiena.
choicesEnglish: Yes, I'll gladly help you.
No, do it yourself, thanks.
I can't, I have a backache.
Wait for another taxi driver.
correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, I'll gladly help you.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Risposta cortese e disponibile.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la aiuto significa I help you.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Polite and helpful response.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, I help you is la aiuto.

choicesItalian: Si fermi qui davanti al cancello.
Si fermi qui vicino al bar.
choicesEnglish: Stop here in front of the gate.
Stop here under the tree.
Stop here near the bar.
Stop here on the corner.
correctAnswerItalian: Si fermi qui davanti al cancello.
correctAnswerEnglish: Stop here in front of the gate.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Specificare il punto esatto di arrivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Specifying the exact point of arrival.

choicesItalian: Può abbassare il volume della radio?
Può spegnere la radio del tutto?
Può alzare il volume, mi piace!
choicesEnglish: Can you lower the radio volume?
Can you change the radio station?
Can you turn off the radio completely?
Can you turn up the volume, I like it!
correctAnswerItalian: Può abbassare il volume della radio?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you lower the radio volume?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Richiesta per un viaggio più tranquillo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Request for a quieter ride.

choicesItalian: Qual è il numero della centrale?
Qual è il nome del tassista?
Qual è il costo del taxi?
choicesEnglish: What is the dispatch number?
What is the taxi driver's name?
What is the taxi cost?
What is the flight time?
correctAnswerItalian: Qual è il numero della centrale?
correctAnswerEnglish: What is the dispatch number?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Informazione utile per prenotazioni future.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, numero della centrale significa dispatch number.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Useful information for future bookings.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, dispatch number is numero della centrale.

choicesItalian: È il mio numero privato, eccolo.
choicesEnglish: It's 06 3535, they answer right away.
It's 113, call in an emergency.
It's 800 123 456, it's free.
It's my private number, here it is.
correctAnswerEnglish: It's 06 3535, they answer right away.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Fornire il recapito telefonico richiesto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, risponde col numero della centrale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Providing the requested phone number.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he answers with the dispatch number.

choicesItalian: Mi può dare una ricevuta, per favore?
Mi può dare un biglietto, per favore?
Mi può dare un resto, per favore?
Mi può dare un aiuto, per favore?
choicesEnglish: Can you give me a receipt, please?
Can you give me a ticket, please?
Can you give me some change, please?
Can you give me some help, please?
correctAnswerItalian: Mi può dare una ricevuta, per favore?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you give me a receipt, please?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Richiesta formale a fine corsa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Formal request at the end of the ride.

choicesItalian: No, ho finito la carta oggi.
Sì, ma costa un euro in più.
choicesEnglish: Certainly, I'll make it immediately.
No, I ran out of paper today.
Yes, but it costs one euro more.
Look for it on the back seat.
correctAnswerEnglish: Certainly, I'll make it immediately.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Disponibilità del tassista.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The taxi driver's availability.

choicesEnglish: We have arrived at the destination.
We have left from the airport.
We are a little late.
We are still in traffic.
correctAnswerEnglish: We have arrived at the destination.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Annuncio della fine del viaggio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Announcement of the end of the trip.

choicesEnglish: Can you open the trunk?
Can you open the door?
Can you open the window?
Can you open the bag?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you open the trunk?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per poter prendere le valigie.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To be able to get the suitcases.

choicesEnglish: Here you go, goodbye.
Here you go, good morning.
Here you go, good night.
Here you go, have a good trip.
correctAnswerEnglish: Here you go, goodbye.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Saluto finale cordiale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Cordial final greeting.

choicesEnglish: Thank you very much, very kind.
Thank you so much, very good.
Thank you dear, very beautiful.
Thank you friend, very strong.
correctAnswerEnglish: Thank you very much, very kind.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un ringraziamento sincero.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A sincere thank you.

choicesEnglish: Is there a night surcharge?
Is there a holiday surcharge?
Is there a luggage surcharge?
Is there a speed surcharge?
correctAnswerEnglish: C'è un supplemento notturno?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Informarsi su eventuali costi extra.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Inquiring about any extra costs.

choicesEnglish: Stop at number twenty.
Stop at number one hundred.
Stop at number ten.
Stop at number two.
correctAnswerEnglish: Stop at number twenty.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Indicare il numero civico preciso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Indicating the precise street number.

choicesEnglish: Do you accept smartphone payments?
Do you accept check payments?
Do you accept coin payments?
Do you accept grocery payments?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you accept smartphone payments?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Verificare le opzioni di pagamento digitale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Verifying digital payment options.

choicesItalian: Sì, tramite Apple Pay o Google Pay.
No, solo con la carta fisica.
Sì, ma solo per piccole cifre.
Non so cosa sia uno smartphone.
choicesEnglish: Yes, via Apple Pay or Google Pay.
No, only with a physical card.
Yes, but only for small amounts.
I don't know what a smartphone is.
correctAnswerItalian: Sì, tramite Apple Pay o Google Pay.
correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, via Apple Pay or Google Pay.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Conferma delle opzioni moderne di pagamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Confirmation of modern payment options.

choicesItalian: Qual è la via più veloce?
Qual è la via più lunga?
Qual è la via più bella?
Qual è la via più cara?
choicesEnglish: Which is the fastest way?
Which is the longest way?
Which is the most beautiful way?
Which is the most expensive way?
correctAnswerItalian: Qual è la via più veloce?
correctAnswerEnglish: Which is the fastest way?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere l'itinerario migliore per risparmiare tempo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for the best itinerary to save time.

choicesItalian: La via interna è sempre chiusa.
La piazza è troppo affollata ora.
choicesEnglish: The highway is usually the best one.
The inner road is always closed.
The square is too crowded now.
The trail is impassable today.
correctAnswerEnglish: The highway is usually the best one.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Suggerimento sulla strada principale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Suggestion on the main road.

choicesEnglish: Is it possible to smoke in the taxi?
Is it possible to eat in the taxi?
Is it possible to sleep in the taxi?
Is it possible to sing in the taxi?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is it possible to smoke in the taxi?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere il permesso per un comportamento specifico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking permission for a specific behavior.

choicesItalian: Sì, ma solo con il finestrino aperto.
Non lo so, non è mio il taxi.
choicesEnglish: No, it is strictly forbidden.
Yes, you can do as you wish.
Yes, but only with the window open.
I don't know, it's not my taxi.
correctAnswerEnglish: No, it is strictly forbidden.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Regola chiara di comportamento a bordo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Clear rule of behavior on board.

choicesItalian: Quanto dista la stazione da qui?
Quanto dista il parco dalla piazza?
choicesEnglish: How far is the center from the airport?
How far is the sea from the hotel?
How far is the station from here?
How far is the park from the square?
correctAnswerEnglish: How far is the center from the airport?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere la distanza geografica.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for geographic distance.

choicesEnglish: It's about thirty kilometers.
It's about ten meters.
It's about a hundred steps.
It's about two hours.
correctAnswerEnglish: It's about thirty kilometers.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una stima della distanza chilometrica.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An estimate of the kilometric distance.

choicesItalian: Può andare un po' più piano?
Può andare un po' più veloce?
Può andare un po' più dritto?
Può andare un po' più lontano?
choicesEnglish: Can you go a bit slower?
Can you go a bit faster?
Can you go a bit straighter?
Can you go a bit further?
correctAnswerItalian: Può andare un po' più piano?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you go a bit slower?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere di moderare la velocità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking to moderate the speed.

choicesItalian: Sì, ma costa di più allora.
choicesEnglish: Certainly, sorry for the rush.
No, I must arrive right away.
Yes, but it costs more then.
I can't, there is traffic.
correctAnswerEnglish: Certainly, sorry for the rush.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Risposta accomodante del tassista.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Accommodating response from the taxi driver.

choicesEnglish: Can you wake me up when we arrive?
Can you call me when we arrive?
Can you tell me when we arrive?
Can you greet me when we arrive?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you wake me up when we arrive?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per chi vuole riposare durante il tragitto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! For those who want to rest during the ride.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: Wrong. To wake up is svegliare.

choicesItalian: Sì, non si preoccupi, riposi pure.
No, non è permesso dormire qui.
Sì, ma solo se russa forte.
choicesEnglish: Yes, don't worry, feel free to rest.
No, it is not allowed to sleep here.
Yes, but only if you snore loudly.
Do it yourself, thanks.
correctAnswerItalian: Sì, non si preoccupi, riposi pure.
correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, don't worry, feel free to rest.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Risposta gentile e rassicurante.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Kind and reassuring response.

choicesItalian: Grazie per il viaggio e per la chiacchierata.
Grazie per il pranzo e per il vino.
Grazie per il libro e per la storia.
Grazie per il regalo e per la festa.
choicesEnglish: Thanks for the ride and for the chat.
Thanks for the lunch and for the wine.
Thanks for the book and for the story.
Thanks for the gift and for the party.
correctAnswerItalian: Grazie per il viaggio e per la chiacchierata.
correctAnswerEnglish: Thanks for the ride and for the chat.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un saluto amichevole a fine corsa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A friendly greeting at the end of the ride.
